skill.name: gws-sheets-append
skill.description: Google Sheets: Append a row to a spreadsheet.
skill.summary: Google Sheets: Append a row to a spreadsheet.
skill.author: googleworkspace

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution and path-traversal alerts are mostly Markdown false positives from examples and relative documentation links. The real risk is that this community skill instructs the assistant to run a Google Workspace CLI command that writes rows to a spreadsheet, so publication is acceptable with a user-confirmation warning.

medium_findings.title: Spreadsheet Write Operation Through External CLI
medium_findings.description: The skill documents use of the gws command to append rows to a Google Sheets spreadsheet. This is an intended capability, but it can modify cloud data and therefore requires explicit user confirmation before execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and the file states that this is a write command. The risk is operational rather than malicious because the caution requires user confirmation.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Flagged as Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported shell backtick execution, but the matching content is Markdown formatting around usage examples and flag names. No executable script, dynamic command construction, or code runner appears in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relevant lines are inside Markdown code fences, table cells, or inline code spans. There is no Ruby code or shell execution primitive in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Relative Documentation Links Flagged as Path Traversal
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported path traversal because the skill references sibling skill documentation with ../ paths. These are Markdown links and prerequisite reading instructions, not file access code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The ../ sequences are present only in Markdown documentation references. No file read, file write, or path concatenation behavior is implemented in this skill file.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Alert Not Supported by Evidence
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptographic algorithm patterns at the description line and flag table header. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, or any cryptographic operation in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain a plain description and a Markdown table header. They do not contain cryptographic API names or algorithm selections.

content.value_statement: Manual spreadsheet entry slows down repeated reporting and data collection tasks. This skill helps Claude, Codex, or Claude Code append rows to Google Sheets through the Google Workspace CLI.
actual_capabilities: Append a single row to a Google Sheets spreadsheet.
Append multiple rows by using JSON-formatted row values.
Target a specific sheet tab with an A1 notation range.
Use a spreadsheet ID supplied by the user.
Remind the assistant to confirm before running the write command.
limitations: Requires the gws command-line tool and Google Workspace authentication.
Does not define spreadsheet creation or permission management steps.
Accepts simple comma-separated values or JSON row arrays only.
Can modify spreadsheet data, so user confirmation is required.

use_cases.title: Add Sales Entries
use_cases.description: Append new sales records to a shared tracking spreadsheet after reviewing the values with the user.

use_cases.title: Log Research Results
use_cases.description: Insert cleaned research rows into a Google Sheet used for analysis or handoff.

use_cases.title: Update Project Trackers
use_cases.description: Add new task, status, or milestone rows to a team spreadsheet without opening the browser.

prompt_templates.title: Append One Row
prompt_templates.prompt: Append one row to my Google Sheet. Use spreadsheet ID SPREADSHEET_ID and values: Alice, 100, true. Confirm before running the write command.

prompt_templates.title: Append to a Sheet Tab
prompt_templates.prompt: Append this row to the Sales tab in spreadsheet SPREADSHEET_ID: Alice, 100. Use the correct A1 range and ask me to confirm first.

prompt_templates.title: Append Multiple Rows
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare a multi-row append for spreadsheet SPREADSHEET_ID using these rows: first row a and b, second row c and d. Show the rows before execution.

prompt_templates.title: Validate and Append Data
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these candidate rows for consistent column counts, then append them to Sheet2 in spreadsheet SPREADSHEET_ID after I approve the final values.

output_examples.input: Append Alice, 100, true to my spreadsheet.
output_examples.output: The assistant confirms the spreadsheet ID and row values, then prepares a Google Workspace CLI append operation for approval.

output_examples.input: Add two rows to Sheet2 with the values a, b and c, d.
output_examples.output: The assistant formats the two rows for bulk insertion, targets Sheet2, and asks for confirmation before modifying the spreadsheet.

output_examples.input: Log this project milestone in the shared tracker.
output_examples.output: The assistant maps the milestone fields to a spreadsheet row and appends it after the user confirms the destination.

best_practices: Confirm the spreadsheet ID, target tab, and row values before execution.
Use JSON row arrays when appending multiple rows or values containing commas.
Keep a clear record of what data was appended and when.
anti_patterns: Do not append sensitive data to a spreadsheet without explicit user approval.
Do not guess a spreadsheet ID or sheet tab when the destination is unclear.
Do not use comma-separated values when cell text itself contains commas.

faq.question: Does this skill create new spreadsheets?
faq.answer: No. It appends rows to an existing Google Sheets spreadsheet.

faq.question: Can it append more than one row?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the multi-row JSON values option for bulk inserts.

faq.question: Can it choose a specific sheet tab?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide an A1 notation range, such as a tab name followed by A1.

faq.question: Does it require Google authentication?
faq.answer: Yes. The Google Workspace CLI must be installed and authenticated.

faq.question: Is this a read-only skill?
faq.answer: No. It writes data by appending rows, so confirmation is required.

faq.question: Can Claude Code or Codex use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
